title: Enforce isolation at the data layer, not just the app
description: Back application-level scoping with row-level security or equivalent so a missed filter in code cannot expose another tenant's data.
body: Use when the database supports policy-based access and the data is sensitive. Do not rely on every query author remembering the tenant filter. Actions: enable row-level security with a policy keyed on the session's tenant context; set the tenant context from the authenticated principal per request; keep app-level scoping too as defense in depth. Validation signals include the database rejecting a query whose tenant context does not match the row. Risk boundary: app-only scoping fails the moment one query forgets the predicate, so add a data-layer guard and escalate any path that bypasses it.
